session_participant_callbacks_t(3) Library Functions Manual session_participant_callbacks_t(3)

NAME

session_participant_callbacks_t - Callback function prototypes for session participant events.

SYNOPSIS

#include <src/common/session/participant.h>

Data Fields


void(* on_connected )(session_participant_t *p, uint32_t client_id, void *user_data)
Called when successfully connected to session. void(* on_disconnected )(session_participant_t *p, void *user_data)
Called when disconnected from session. void(* on_frame_received )(session_participant_t *p, const char *frame, void *user_data)
Called when ASCII frame received from server. void(* on_audio_received )(session_participant_t *p, const float *samples, size_t count, void *user_data)
Called when audio samples received from server. void(* on_settings_changed )(session_participant_t *p, const session_settings_t *settings, void *user_data)
Called when session settings change. void(* on_error )(session_participant_t *p, asciichat_error_t error, const char *message, void *user_data)
Called when an error occurs.

Detailed Description

Callback function prototypes for session participant events.

Definition at line 77 of file participant.h.

Field Documentation

void(* session_participant_callbacks_t::on_audio_received) (session_participant_t *p, const float *samples, size_t count, void *user_data)

Called when audio samples received from server.

Parameters

p Participant handle
samples Audio sample buffer (float format)
count Number of samples
user_data User-provided context pointer

Definition at line 108 of file participant.h.

void(* session_participant_callbacks_t::on_connected) (session_participant_t *p, uint32_t client_id, void *user_data)

Called when successfully connected to session.

Parameters

p Participant handle
client_id Assigned client ID from server
user_data User-provided context pointer

p Participant handle
error Error code
message Error message
user_data User-provided context pointer

Called when ASCII frame received from server.

p Participant handle
frame ASCII frame data (null-terminated)
user_data User-provided context pointer
